content: Quick note from professional review of Cohort Week 8 beta: the NRS slider currently has no verbal anchors at 0 and 10. Per WCAG 2.1 and Farrar et al. 2001, this is both an accessibility gap and a measurement validity concern. '0 = no pain' and '10 = worst pain imaginable' should appear on-screen, not just in onboarding text.
Totally agree, Frank. Adding to cohort action item and flagging as compliance. Can you add a spec comment in the Jira with the exact Farrar citation? Makes it easier for Lisa to implement correctly.

Also worth noting from the usability testing - two Cohort Participants said they weren't sure if '5' meant moderate pain or halfway to their personal worst. Verbal anchors would resolve this. +1 to Frank's feedback.

Just got off the phone with Dr. Tran. He's ready to move forward with the Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group - 3 OTs (me, Marcus Yee, Carla Nair) and an initial cohort of ~10 participants with chronic pain group topics. He'd like to start onboarding the OTs in July and run a 6-week pilot through August. Main ask: can we have therapist account provisioning and the professional dashboard ready by July 14?

This is huge - BRC is exactly the kind of credible professional partner we need for fundraising. July 14 is tight but doable if we deprioritize CLINDASH-51. @Rowan can you confirm the dashboard API is stable enough for a limited pilot?

Sharing the Moseley & Butler 2013 'Explain Pain' framework - this is the core theoretical underpinning of our pacing approach. Key for HTCC exam too. https://www.noigroup.com/product/explain-pain-second-edition/ - I have a summary note in my Obsidian vault I can share as a PDF if anyone wants.

Yes please, Frank! Also - have you looked at the goal measure as an intake measure for the occupation-based cohort intake steps? CHT literature shows strong face validity for chronic pain group populations.

Pre-read for July 14 Q3 advisory call uploaded to our shared OneDrive folder. My three agenda items: (1) adaptive baseline UX - is the ACT-based variable capacity framing clear to participants? (2) occupation-based onboarding - goal measure vs. custom goal-setting screen; (3) partner dashboard v2 priority features. Please add comments by July 10.
Cook night was a success - Japanese curry with karaage. Sasha's dashi was incredible. I'll post the modified recipe to the shared Google Drive. Highly recommend the kewpie mayo on the karaage.
Jealous. Someone please save me some curry next time. What's the next cook night theme?

Standup 2026-03-10 (async): Yesterday - reviewed Cohort Week 8 beta feedback digest, added professional spec comments to cohort action item and cohort action item in Jira. Today - drafting Q1 Chapter Advisory agenda, meeting Dr. Tran at BRC re: pilot timeline. Blockers - waiting on therapist account provisioning spec from Rowan.

Standup 2026-03-10 (async): Yesterday - investor deck review with Community Partner Onboarding advisor, reviewed Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group contract draft. Today - co-working session with Willa on Q3 roadmap, follow-up with OHSU on pilot timeline. Blockers - none.

Q3 roadmap draft is in Notion. Top priorities: (1) adaptive baseline GA - targeting Aug 1, (2) occupation-based onboarding v1 - targeting Sept 1, (3) BRC professional dashboard - supporting Frank's pilot. Frank, your input on priority order between items 2 and 3 would be really valuable.

My vote: BRC dashboard should be priority 2 because it unblocks a real professional pilot and gives us outcome data before the MTOTA conference in September. Occupation-based onboarding is strategically important but can ship in Sept without delaying the pilot. Adaptive baseline GA is non-negotiable for Aug.

MTOTA conference update: Rowan McAllister confirmed Finthesiss.ai can present a 20-min session on occupation-centered Chronic Pain Program at the September Montana Occupational Therapy conference. This is a huge visibility opportunity - I'll co-present with Sasha. Rowan needs a session abstract by July 1.

Big news - Bitterroot Rehabilitation Center has signed our professional pilot agreement! This is our first real-world professional deployment. Huge thanks to Frank for making this connection and shepherding it through Dr. Tran's professional review. Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group starts July 2026.

pacing worksheet v2.4.1 deployed to prod. Adaptive baseline is live for beta users. Frank, can you do a final professional walkthrough this week and confirm the capacity variability framing reads correctly to a participant? Screenshots in the #professional-ux-feedback channel.

Done - I walked through the full adaptive baseline flow. One professional note: the capacity variability screen says 'your energy may vary day to day' but should frame it as 'your capacity varies - this is normal, not a setback.' The ACT-based framing matters for participant acceptance. I'll log this as cohort action item in Jira.

AOTA Practice Advisory on digital health tools for Occupational Therapy practice dropped this week - relevant to our professional evidence strategy. Frank, I flagged three sections that directly support Finthesiss.ai's approach. Sharing the link in #pain-science-literature.
Thanks Carter - this is exactly what we need for the Bitterroot Chronic Pain Group IRB materials. I'll incorporate the AOTA advisory language into the professional protocol document this week.
